item.title: Yeshnil Karan on track for Glasgow Commonwealth Games after injury recovery
item.description: The Athletics Association says his latest performance at the Gold Coast Marathon 5km race on Saturday provided another encouraging sign of his progress. They say Karan clocked 15:00 to finish 11th overall in a quality field, running confidently with the lead pack and covering the opening 3,000 metres in a promising 8:43.
item.content:encoded:
The Athletics Association says his latest performance at the Gold Coast Marathon 5km race on Saturday provided another encouraging sign of his progress.

They say Karan clocked 15:00 to finish 11th overall in a quality field, running confidently with the lead pack and covering the opening 3,000 metres in a promising 8:43.

While he faded slightly over the closing stages, the performance reflected the continued rebuilding of his race-specific endurance and finishing speed.

Coach Atma Maharaj says the result confirmed Karan's recovery remained ahead of schedule.

Maharaj says considering where Karan was just six weeks ago, the progress has been extremely encouraging, and the strong aerobic base he developed before surgery has allowed him to recover remarkably well.

He stressed that there is still important work to do, particularly in rebuilding speed endurance and race sharpness, but he is firmly back on track for Glasgow.

Karan returned to Brisbane four weeks ago, where he is training alongside several of Australia's leading middle and long-distance athletes as he completes the final phase of his Commonwealth Games preparation.

Athletics Fiji says with less than a month remaining before the Games, they are encouraged by Karan's continued progress and remains confident he is building towards peak condition ahead of representing Fiji on the international stage in Glasgow.

item.title: De Ketelaere double gives Belgium 2-1 advantage over USA at halftime
item.description: Belgium head into the halftime break with a 2-1 lead over the United States in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash in Seattle Stadium. Charles De Ketelaere has been the hero for the Red Devils, scoring twice in the ninth and 33rd minutes to put Belgium in command.
item.content:encoded:
Belgium head into the halftime break with a 2-1 lead over the United States in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash in Seattle Stadium.

Charles De Ketelaere has been the hero for the Red Devils, scoring twice in the ninth and 33rd minutes to put Belgium in command.

The United States fought back through Malik Tillman, who found the back of the net in the 31st minute to briefly level the contest before Belgium regained the lead just two minutes later.

A place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals is at stake, with the winner set to face Spain at 7am on Saturday.

Spain booked their place in the last eight earlier this morning after edging Portugal 1-0 in their Round of 16 encounter.

Meanwhile, in tomorrow's remaining Round of 16 matches, Argentina will take on Egypt at 4am, while Switzerland will face Colombia at 8am.

Stay with us for updates on the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Vakatawa, Tagi step in as Radradra and Hoyt injured
item.description: Former French international rugby player Virimi Vakatawa and Bayon-based prop Luke Tagi will join the FIJI Water Flying Fijians camp, replacing Semi Radradra and Tim Hoyt. Tagi, who was not available for selection earlier, is set to return, and Vakatawa will likely make a debut soon after an impressive game against South Africa and Wales with the Barbarians team.
item.content:encoded:
Former French international rugby player Virimi Vakatawa and Bayon-based prop Luke Tagi will join the FIJI Water Flying Fijians camp, replacing Semi Radradra and Tim Hoyt.

Tagi, who was not available for selection earlier, is set to return, and Vakatawa will likely make a debut soon after an impressive game against South Africa and Wales with the Barbarians team.

Fiji's hopes of a winning start to the tour were hampered by ill-discipline, conceding too many penalties that allowed Wales to take control of the contest.

The Flying Fijians will now look to improve their discipline and reduce their penalty count as they prepare for their second Test.

Fiji face England at Hill Dickinson Stadium on Sunday, with kick-off at 1.10am Fiji time.

In other games this week, New Zealand takes on Italy at 5.10pm at the Sky Stadium, Australia faces France at 7.40pm at the Brisbane Stadium, and Ireland meets Japan at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will face Scotland at 3.40am in Pretoria and Wales will play Argentina at 7.10am at San Juan.

item.title: Merino’s late winner sends Spain into FIFA World Cup quarter-finals
item.description: Spain will now face the winner of the USA and Belgium last-16 clash in the quarter-finals on Saturday at 7am. Meanwhile, the last-16 match between the USA and Belgium kicks off at 12pm today.
item.content:encoded:
Spain will now face the winner of the USA and Belgium last-16 clash in the quarter-finals on Saturday at 7am.

Meanwhile, the last-16 match between the USA and Belgium kicks off at 12pm today.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ronaldo confirms 2026 FIFA World Cup will be his last
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo confirms that the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the last of his career.At 41, the Portugal captain is set to make

item.title: Portugal and Spain set for blockbuster World Cup last 16 clash
item.description: Portugal and Spain will renew their football rivalry in the next hour as they face off in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash, with a quarterfinal spot on the line. Portugal head into the knockout match after edging Croatia 2-1 in the Round of 32, while Spain made a major statement with a commanding 3-0 win over Austria.
item.content:encoded:
Portugal and Spain will renew their football rivalry in the next hour as they face off in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash, with a quarterfinal spot on the line.

Portugal head into the knockout match after edging Croatia 2-1 in the Round of 32, while Spain made a major statement with a commanding 3-0 win over Austria.

The spotlight will again fall on Portugal captain Cristiano Ronaldo, who is featuring at his final World Cup, while Spain will look to young star Lamine Yamal to provide the attacking spark.

Spain have also carried impressive defensive form into the clash, having not conceded in their first four matches of the tournament.

There is plenty of history between the neighbouring nations, with their last World Cup meeting producing a memorable 3-all draw in 2018, when Ronaldo scored a hat-trick.

The winner will advance to face either the United States or Belgium in the quarterfinals.

item.title: Nasinu Secondary dominate Deans and Raluve round 5 competition
item.description: After Round 5 of the Vodafone Southern Zone Deans Competition, Nasinu Secondary School continued their dominant run across all grades and the Weet-Bix Raluve category, remaining unbeaten in all their matches. In the Under-15 grade, they drew Marist Brothers High School 14-14 in a closely contested encounter.
item.content:encoded:
After Round 5 of the Vodafone Southern Zone Deans Competition, Nasinu Secondary School continued their dominant run across all grades and the Weet-Bix Raluve category, remaining unbeaten in all their matches.

In the Under-15 grade, they drew Marist Brothers High School 14-14 in a closely contested encounter.

Their Under-16 team then delivered a strong performance, defeating Rampur College 22–10.

The Under-18 side put on a commanding display, overwhelming Ratu Sir Lala Sukuna Memorial School 41–7, while the Under-19 team secured a solid 17–12 win over Assemblies of God High School.

In the Weet-Bix Southern Zone Raluve Competition, Nasinu’s Under-16 team cruised past Lomaivuna with a convincing 19–0 victory, while their Under-18 Raluve side had a bye.

Nasinu will be aiming to maintain their dominance in this week’s round of the competition.

The quarter-finals will be held this weekend.

item.title: We were unsettled at times and it was very disappointing - Seruvakula
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Coach Senirusi Seruvakula has given full credit to the Wales team, saying they were unsettled at times and it was very disappointing. Seruvakula made these comments to the media after the Flying Fijians went down 35-24 in their Nations Championship opener in Cardiff yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Coach Senirusi Seruvakula has given full credit to the Wales team, saying they were unsettled at times and it was very disappointing.

Seruvakula made these comments to the media after the Flying Fijians went down 35-24 in their Nations Championship opener in Cardiff yesterday.

Insert: Seruvakula on opportunities July 6

He says he thought that they were going to play the second half the way they wanted, but unfortunately things did not go their way.

When asked what they will change in their upcoming games against England and Scotland, Seruvakula says they will work on their collisions, support play, setpieces, and especially their scrums.

When questioned about offloads in the second half, the Acting Flying Fijians coach says its the way Fiji plays, but at times they pushed it too far.

Captain Tevita Ikanivere says they need to find a balance when the game slows down, be a bit more patient and for their forwards to just grind it out.

The Flying Fijians play England at 1.10am Sunday at Hill Dickinson Stadium in Liverpool.

In other games, New Zealand will face Italy at 5.10pm Saturday, Australia host France at 7.40pm, Japan will meet Ireland at 10.10pm, South Africa plays Scotland at 3.40am Sunday, while Argentina will meet Wales at 7.10am.

item.title: England survive Mexico fightback to seal 3-2 win and quarter-final spot
item.description: England have survived a dramatic Mexico fightback to seal a thrilling 3-2 win with 10 men and book their place in the FIFA World C

item.title: Neymar Jr retires after World Cup exit
item.description: An emotional Neymar says his career with Brazil had "finished" after his team's World Cup exit against Norway in the round of 16 e

item.title: Haaland double fires Norway past Brazil into historic quarter-final
item.description: Norway has reached the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals for the first time with a dramatic 2-1 victory over Brazil, inspired by a sen

item.title: Penalty count proves costly as Flying Fijians fall 39-24 to Wales
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians' penalty count proved costly as they went down 39-24 to Wales, allowing Wales to take control of the

item.title: Drua Women secure home semi-final in Lautoka after finishing top of Super Rugby Women’s standings
item.description: The McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women have secured a home semi-final and clinched the minor premiership after finishing at the top of the

item.title: 16-year-old New Zealand-based Somit Maharaj earns Junior Bula Boys call-up
item.description: A proud father who once dreamt of playing football for Fiji but never got the opportunity says that dream is now being fulfilled t
